# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

node('linux') {
    stage('Pull Artifacts') {
        buildArtifactUrl = "${params.build_url}" // URL of the Jenkins job which builds the artifact
        archivePath = "/var/lib/jenkins/workspace/jobName/archive" 

        dir(archivePath) {
            git url: 'ssh://git@gitlab.example.com/group/project.git', branch: 'main' // replace with your SCM URL and branch
            sh "wget ${buildArtifactUrl}/path_to_artifact"  // Replace '/path_to_artifact' with the actual path to the artifact in the build job, relative or absolute depending on the Jenkins server configuration. 
        }
    }
    
    stage('Deploy to Production') {
        servers = ['server1', 'server2'] // Replace these with your production servers
        
        for (int i = 0; i < servers.size(); i++) {
            server = servers[i]
            sshagent(['jenkins-key']) {  // Replace 'jenkins-key' with the credentials ID which has access to production servers
                sh "scp -r ${archivePath} user@${server}:/path_to_deploy" // replace '/path_to_deploy' with the actual path where you want to deploy on the server. It could be anywhere inside that server depending upon your requirements. 
            }
        }
    }
}
